content: who is jon snow's real father

I cannot find this in the documents. The provided context does not explicitly state who Jon Snow's real father is. While [GOT BOOK 1.pdf | Page 55] confirms that Lord Eddard Stark is Jon's father, it is widely implied in the series that this may not be the case, and that Jon's true parentage may be different. However, the documents provided do not offer any conclusive evidence or information about Jon Snow's real father.
ask("Who is jon snow")

Jon Snow is referred to as "a son of Winterfell, a nephew of Benjen Stark" [GOT BOOK 3.pdf | Page 598]. He is also mentioned as "Jon Snow" in various contexts, such as when he is speaking with others [GOT BOOK 4.pdf | Page 349], [GOT BOOK 5.pdf | Page 443], and when others are speaking about him [GOT BOOK 4.pdf | Page 969]. Additionally, it is mentioned that he could potentially become "Jon Stark, the Lord of Winterfell" if he were to pledge himself to Stannis' service [GOT BOOK 4.pdf | Page 969]. However, his true parentage is not explicitly stated in the provided documents.

who is the mother of jon snow

The mother of Jon Snow is not explicitly stated in the documents. However, it is mentioned that if Jon had been born of Ashara Dayne of Starfall, as some whispered, the lady was long dead [GOT BOOK 2.pdf | Page 349]. This suggests that Ashara Dayne is a possible candidate for Jon Snow's mother, but it is not confirmed. Additionally, Catelyn Stark thinks about Jon's mother, wondering if she grieves for Ned as she does, but she has no clue who or where Jon's mother might be [GOT BOOK 2.pdf | Page 349]. Therefore, the documents do not provide a definitive answer to who Jon Snow's mother is.

Ned Stark fever dream Lyanna Tower of Joy kingsguard

In Ned Stark's fever dream, he recalls a memory of the Tower of Joy, where he and six other men, including Howland Reed, fought against three members of the Kingsguard, including Ser Arthur Dayne, the Sword of the Morning, Ser Oswell Whent, and Ser Gerold Hightower, the White Bull [GOT BOOK 1.pdf | Page 388]. The dream suggests that Ned and his companions were trying to reach Lyanna, who was inside the tower, and that the Kingsguard were trying to prevent them from doing so. The dream also implies that the event at the Tower of Joy was a traumatic and significant one for Ned, and that it has had a lasting impact on him [GOT BOOK 1.pdf | Page 391]. Additionally, it is mentioned that Rhaegar had named the place the "tower of joy", but for Ned it was a bitter memory [GOT BOOK 1.pdf | Page 391].

The dream suggests that Ned and his companions were trying to reach Lyanna, who was inside the tower, and that the Kingsguard were trying to prevent them from doing so. The dream also implies that the event at the Tower of Joy was a traumatic and significant one for Ned, and that it has had a lasting impact on him [GOT BOOK 1.pdf | Page 391]. Additionally, it is mentioned that Rhaegar had named the place the "tower of joy", but for Ned it was a bitter memory [GOT BOOK 1.pdf | Page 391]. It is also stated that only two of the seven men who fought at the Tower of Joy survived: Eddard Stark himself and the little crannogman, Howland Reed [GOT BOOK 1.pdf | Page 391].
who is arya stark

Arya Stark is a member of House Stark, the daughter of Lord Eddard Stark and Lady Catelyn Stark [GOT BOOK 1.pdf | Page 204]. She is also known as Arya Underfoot [GOT BOOK 4.pdf | Page 189] and has been referred to as Arya Horseface by her sister Sansa [GOT BOOK 5.pdf | Page 413]. Arya is a highborn girl who has been on the run and has assumed various identities, including being a serving boy and a cupbearer [GOT BOOK 4.pdf | Page 189]. She is known for her bravery, determination, and skill with a sword, as well as her ability to adapt to different situations and personas [GOT BOOK 1.pdf | Page 492]. Arya has also been mentioned as the potential Lady of Winterfell and the wife of a lord [GOT BOOK 5.pdf | Page 146]. Throughout the documents, Arya's character is portrayed as strong-willed and independent, often finding herself in difficult and dangerous situations [GOT BOOK 3.pdf | Page 209].

what happened to the Others and the White Walkers

According to the documents, the Others and the White Walkers were defeated in a battle known as the Battle for the Dawn, which took place at the end of the Long Night [the-world-of-ice-fire.pdf | Page 32]. The battle was fought by the first men of the Night's Watch, who were able to defeat the Others with the help of the children of the forest. The Others were forced to flee to the icy north, and the Long Night came to an end [the-world-of-ice-fire.pdf | Page 32]. However, the documents do not provide a detailed account of the final battle or the fate of the Night King, the leader of the White Walkers. 

It is also mentioned that the Others were said to have been defeated by a last hero who sought out the intercession of the children of the forest, and that the first men of the Night's Watch were able to fight and win the Battle for the Dawn [the-world-of-ice-fire.pdf | Page 32]. Additionally, Old Nan tells Bran a story about the Others, saying that they were cold things, dead things, that hated iron and fire and the touch of the sun, and that they swept over holdfasts and cities and kingdoms, felling heroes and armies by the score [GOT BOOK 1.pdf | Page 225]. 

However, the documents do not provide a clear or detailed account of what happened to the Others and the White Walkers, or how they were ultimately defeated. The information provided is largely based on legend and stories passed down through the years, and it is not clear what is fact and what is myth [GOT BOOK 1.pdf | Page 225], [the-world-of-ice-fire.pdf | Page 32].
who killed the Night King

Arya Stark killed the Night King by plunging a dagger made out of Valyrian steel into his chest [got_wiki.txt | Page 1]. This event occurred during the Great War, when the Night King and his army of the dead were marching towards Winterfell, and Arya ambushed him in the Godswood, stabbing him with the dagger and causing him and the army of the dead to disintegrate [got_wiki.txt | Page 1].

what is the fate of Jon Snow

⚠️ This is not in the books yet — the following is from the TV show only:
In the TV series, Jon Snow's fate is that he is sentenced to the Night's Watch by Bran, the new King of the Six Kingdoms, as a compromise to appease Daenerys' supporters after Jon kills Daenerys to protect the people [got_wiki.txt | Page 1]. Jon returns to Castle Black and leads the wildlings to return to their lands beyond the Wall [got_wiki.txt | Page 1]. 

However, in the books, Jon Snow's fate is still uncertain. He is stabbed by mutineers from the Night's Watch in the fifth book, but his ultimate fate is not yet known [GOT BOOK 5.pdf | Page 652]. The author, George R. R. Martin, has not commented on Jon's fate, saying "Oh, you think he’s dead, do you?" when asked about Jon's death [got_wiki.txt | Page 1].
who ends up on the Iron Throne

⚠️ This is not in the books yet — the following is from the TV show only:
Bran Stark is declared the King of the Six Kingdoms, with the title Bran the Broken, after a vote by the lords and ladies of Westeros [got_wiki.txt | Page 1]. He appoints Tyrion Lannister as his Hand of the King to compensate for the mistakes he made as Daenerys's Hand [got_wiki.txt | Page 1]. Sansa Stark, however, requests the North's independence, which Bran accepts, making her the Queen in the North [got_wiki.txt | Page 1].
what is dragonglass and how was it used against the dead

Dragonglass, also known as obsidian, is a volcanic glass that is anathema to the White Walkers and the wights [GOT BOOK 3.pdf | Page 730]. According to the maesters, it comes from the fires of the earth [GOT BOOK 4.pdf | Page 426]. The children of the forest used to give the Night's Watch a hundred obsidian daggers every year during the Age of Heroes [GOT BOOK 5.pdf | Page 89]. Samwell Tarly discovered that dragonglass can shatter when used against a wight, but it is effective against them [GOT BOOK 3.pdf | Page 730]. Jon Snow found hundreds of arrowheads and spearheads made of dragonglass on the Fist [GOT BOOK 4.pdf | Page 426]. The Night's Watch believes that dragonglass is a crucial weapon in the fight against the White Walkers and the wights, and they are trying to find more of it to arm themselves [GOT BOOK 4.pdf | Page 426]. Melisandre, the red priestess, also refers to dragonglass as "frozen fire" and notes that it is effective against the wights, but not against the Others [GOT BOOK 3.pdf | Page 730].
who is jon snow

⚠️ This is not in the books yet — the following is from the TV show only:
Jon Snow is a fictional main character in the A Song of Ice and Fire series of epic fantasy novels by American author George R. R. Martin, and its HBO television adaptation Game of Thrones, in which he is portrayed by Kit Harington [got_wiki.txt | Page 1]. He is introduced as the 14-year-old illegitimate son of Eddard "Ned" Stark, Lord of Winterfell, and half-brother to Robb, Sansa, Arya, Bran, and Rickon [got_wiki.txt | Page 1]. Jon is described as having strong Stark features with a lean build, long face, dark brown hair, and dark grey eyes [got_wiki.txt | Page 1]. He joins the Night's Watch and becomes a key character in the series, eventually becoming the Lord Commander of the Night's Watch [got_wiki.txt | Page 1]. Jon's true parentage is later revealed to be the son of Rhaegar Targaryen and Lyanna Stark, making him a Targaryen and a potential heir to the Iron Throne [got_wiki.txt | Page 1].
who is his mother

According to the books, Jon Snow's mother is a topic of speculation and uncertainty. In [GOT BOOK 4.pdf | Page 562], it is mentioned that "Jon has a mother. Wylla, her name is Wylla." However, this information is provided by Edric Dayne, who claims that Wylla was his wetnurse and Jon's mother. 

In [GOT BOOK 2.pdf | Page 349], Catelyn thinks about Jon's mother, wondering if she grieves for Ned or hates him for leaving her, but she does not know who Jon's mother is. It is also mentioned that some people whisper that Jon's mother might be Ashara Dayne, but this is not confirmed.

In [GOT BOOK 2.pdf | Page 522] and [GOT BOOK 5.pdf | Page 828], Lyanna Stark is mentioned as a possible candidate for Jon's mother, but this is not explicitly stated. 

It is worth noting that the book series has not yet revealed the true identity of Jon Snow's mother, and the information provided is based on speculation and rumors within the story.

who is jammie lanister

📖 The books say: Jaime Lannister is a member of House Lannister, and is the son of Lord Tywin and Lady Joanna of Casterly Rock [GOT BOOK 4.pdf | Page 841]. He is also known as the "Kingslayer" for killing King Aerys II Targaryen during the Sack of King's Landing [GOT BOOK 4.pdf | Page 841]. Jaime is a skilled knight and has served in the Kingsguard [GOT BOOK 4.pdf | Page 841]. 

📺 The TV show adds: Jaime Lannister is portrayed by actor Nikolaj Coster-Waldau in the HBO television adaptation Game of Thrones [got_wiki.txt | Page 1]. He is introduced as the 30-year-old twin brother of Queen Cersei Lannister, and is known for his complicated and often conflicted personality [got_wiki.txt | Page 1]. Throughout the series, Jaime's character undergoes significant development, as he navigates his relationships with Cersei, Tyrion, and other characters [got_wiki.txt | Page 1].

What is the Iron Throne?

The Iron Throne is a symbol of the ruling power in the Seven Kingdoms of Westeros [Fire & Blood by George R. R. Martin.pdf | Page 36]. It is a great metal seat made from the melted, twisted, beaten, and broken blades of all the fallen foes of Aegon the Conqueror [Fire & Blood by George R. R. Martin.pdf | Page 36]. The throne is described as being full of traps for the unwary, with razor edges and barbs, and is said to have taken a thousand blades to make, heated white-hot in the furnace breath of Balerion the Black Dread [GOT BOOK 1.pdf | Page 426]. The Iron Throne is the seat of power for the ruler of the Seven Kingdoms, and those who sit upon it are considered to be the rightful ruler of the realm [GOT BOOK 4.pdf | Page 1030]. It is said that the throne can kill a man, and has done so in the past, if the stories can be believed [GOT BOOK 1.pdf | Page 426]. The Iron Throne is located in the throne room of the Red Keep in King's Landing, the capital city of the Seven Kingdoms [GOT BOOK 2.pdf | Page 215].
who is arya stark

⚠️ This is not in the books yet — the following is from the TV show only:
Arya Stark is a fictional character in the A Song of Ice and Fire series of epic fantasy novels by American author George R. R. Martin, and its HBO television adaptation Game of Thrones, where she was portrayed by English actress Maisie Williams [got_wiki.txt | Page 1]. She is a prominent point-of-view character in the novels with the third most viewpoint chapters (behind Tyrion Lannister and Jon Snow) and is the only character to have a viewpoint chapter in every published book of the series [got_wiki.txt | Page 1]. Arya is the third child and youngest daughter of Lord Eddard Stark and his wife Lady Catelyn Stark [got_wiki.txt | Page 1]. She is tomboyish, headstrong, feisty, independent, disdains traditional female pursuits, and is often mistaken for a boy [got_wiki.txt | Page 1]. Arya wields a castle-forged steel smallsword named "Needle" forged by Mikken, the blacksmith of Winterfell, as a parting gift from her half-brother Jon Snow, and is trained in the Braavosi style of sword fighting by Syrio Forel [got_wiki.txt | Page 1]. Arya is left-handed and talented in sums and housekeeping [got_wiki.txt | Page 1]. She has five siblings: an older brother Robb, an older sister Sansa, two younger brothers Bran and Rickon, and an older illegitimate half-brother Jon Snow [got_wiki.txt | Page 1]. Arya is the only one out of her full-siblings to inherit the Stark features of lean athletic physique, brown hair, grey eyes and long face [got_wiki.txt | Page 1]. Throughout her travels, Arya displays great resourcefulness and cunning and also becomes increasingly ruthless [got_wiki.txt | Page 1]. Arya can be regarded as an anti heroine, who witnesses both the unjust execution of her father and the immediate aftermath of her mother and brother's murder, and works her way through a revenge death list of names of those responsible for killing all the people she cared about [got_wiki.txt | Page 1]. Arya killing the Night King was nominated for the 2020 BAFTA TV Awards under the "Must-See Moment" category [got_wiki.txt | Page 1].
Tell me about dragons in Westeros

According to the books, dragons were present in Westeros before the Targaryens arrived [the-world-of-ice-fire.pdf | Page 34]. The Valyrians, who were known for their ability to tame and ride dragons, brought their dragons with them when they came to Westeros [the-world-of-ice-fire.pdf | Page 34]. The Targaryens, who were a Valyrian family, used their dragons to conquer and rule Westeros [Fire & Blood by George R. R. Martin.pdf | Page 10]. 

There were several dragons in Westeros during the time of the Targaryen rule, including Balerion, Vhagar, and Meraxes [Fire & Blood by George R. R. Martin.pdf | Page 10]. Balerion, also known as the Black Dread, was the largest and most feared of the Targaryen dragons [GOT BOOK 4.pdf | Page 114]. 

The dragons were used for war and were a symbol of the Targaryen power [GOT BOOK 4.pdf | Page 114]. However, as time passed, the number of dragons in Westeros began to decline, and eventually, they became extinct [Fire & Blood by George R. R. Martin.pdf | Page 535]. 

It is worth noting that, according to the books, dragon bones have been found in various parts of Westeros, including the north and the jungles of Sothoryos [the-world-of-ice-fire.pdf | Page 34]. This suggests that dragons may have been present in Westeros for a long time before the Targaryens arrived. 

In more recent times, Daenerys Targaryen has hatched three dragons, including Drogon, Rhaegal, and Viserion [GOT BOOK 5.pdf | Page 758]. These dragons have grown and become a powerful force in the world of Westeros.
